title: Mark Cuban says AI will transform worker training with job simulators
excerpt: Tech billionaire Mark Cuban says AI will allow future generations of workers to be trained using simulations similar to those used by pilots.
content_html:
Mark Cuban predicts AI will let workers train for jobs using simulators 'like pilots and race car drivers'

By Theron Mohamed

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

Hustling for work experience and learning from coworkers has been key to building a successful career for decades. Mark Cuban says that's bound to change.

The tech billionaire and former "Shark Tank" investor said in an X post on Thursday that he expects the "next great AI application" to be a "job simulator."

"How employees gain experience in a future AI world is going to be far different from today," he wrote.

"Employees won't have as many touch points in the company to gain knowledge and experience from. That's where judgement has historically come from."

Cuban predicted that "much like race car drivers and pilots," future generations of workers will be trained using simulations, intended to teach them how to navigate real-life scenarios in the workplace.

Commercial pilots spend long hours in simulators during their training, while simulators have become increasingly commonplace in recent years for race car drivers to train without being at a race track.

Cuban said that "smart companies will have their employees and stakeholders with the most domain knowledge create the simulator that takes them through every possible situation they could face and helps prepare them."

"Onboarding will have a completely different meaning," he added.

The next great AI application, driven by open source, or open weights, will be a job simulator.

How employees gain experience in a future AI world is going to be far different from today. Employees won’t have as many touch points in the company to gain knowledge and…

— Mark Cuban (@mcuban) July 24, 2026

It's easy to imagine a newly qualified lawyer sitting through simulations of depositions, court hearings, and settlement negotiations so they know what to expect.

Or a trainee doctor preparing for real-life situations by speaking with AI patients, assisting in a virtual surgery, and participating in simulated emergencies.

If, as Cuban suggests, those scenarios are created by harnessing the knowledge and experience of their real-life colleagues at those law firms and hospitals, a job simulator could provide practical, realistic training.

Cuban has posted repeatedly this week that AI will change the world, but falls short in areas such as "reading the room" and having emotional intelligence. He's said that it will work best in collaboration with humans.

"Add AI productivity to the real time capacity and judgement of humans, and you will get the greatest return on both investments," he posted on Thursday.

Theron Mohamed is a London-based correspondent on the Trending team at Business Insider. His coverage spans finance, investing, wealth, markets, and the economy.Theron joined BI in 2019 as a reporter at Markets Insider and rose to the rank of correspondent before moving to the Trending team in 2024. He previously covered tech, media, and telecom stocks for Investors Chronicle magazine and had a brief stint on the Financial Times' Data team. He interned at the Wall Street Journal in New York where he primarily wrote for Heard on the Street.Theron has freelanced for The Independent, The Telegraph, WIRED, and several smaller publications. He holds an undergraduate degree in geography from the London School of Economics, and a master's degree in journalism from Columbia University.Theron often covers Warren Buffett, Michael Burry, Jeremy Grantham and other top-flight investors. He also writes about the world's wealthiest people and shares financial advice from all manner of rich and successful people.Email Theron at tmohamed@businessinsider.com and follow him on X @theron_mohamed.Expertise

blocks.html_en: "Employees won't have as many touch points in the company to gain knowledge and experience from. That's where judgement has historically come from."

blocks.html_en: Commercial pilots spend long hours in simulators during their training, while simulators have become increasingly commonplace in recent years for race car drivers to train without being at a race track.

blocks.html_en: Cuban said that "smart companies will have their employees and stakeholders with the most domain knowledge create the simulator that takes them through every possible situation they could face and helps prepare them."

blocks.html_en: "Onboarding will have a completely different meaning," he added.

sentences.zh: 或者实习医生通过与人工智能患者交谈、协助虚拟手术以及参与模拟紧急情况来为现实生活中的情况做好准备。

blocks.html_en: If, as Cuban suggests, those scenarios are created by harnessing the knowledge and experience of their real-life colleagues at those law firms and hospitals, a job simulator could provide practical, realistic training.

sentences.zh: 如果像库班建议的那样，这些场景是通过利用这些律师事务所和医院现实生活中同事的知识和经验来创建的，那么工作模拟器就可以提供实用、现实的培训。

blocks.html_en: Cuban has posted repeatedly this week that AI will change the world, but falls short in areas such as "reading the room" and having emotional intelligence. He's said that it will work best in collaboration with humans.

sentences.en: Cuban has posted repeatedly this week that AI will change the world, but falls short in areas such as "reading the room" and having emotional intelligence.
sentences.zh: 库班本周多次表示，人工智能将改变世界，但在“阅读房间”和情商等领域存在不足。

sentences.en: He's said that it will work best in collaboration with humans.
sentences.zh: 他说它与人类合作效果最好。

blocks.html_en: "Add AI productivity to the real time capacity and judgement of humans, and you will get the greatest return on both investments," he posted on Thursday.

sentences.en: "Add AI productivity to the real time capacity and judgement of humans, and you will get the greatest return on both investments," he posted on Thursday.
sentences.zh: “将人工智能生产力与人类的实时能力和判断力相结合，你将从这两项投资中获得最大的回报，”他在周四发帖称。

sentences.zh: 热门文章 阿尔·帕西诺说，他从 5000 万美元到破产，加入了一长串经历过金钱困境的明星名单 一位石油大亨今年以 260 亿美元的价格出售了自己的公司，但在交易完成前就去世了 沃伦·巴菲特每天喝 5 罐可乐——这就是他在近 50 年后从百事可乐离职的原因 来认识一下 1000 亿美元俱乐部中的 16 个人——他们的身价加起来比亚马逊或谷歌“大空头”投资者迈克尔·伯里 (Michael Burry) 的价值还要高安静，大举进军中国科技行业，并在 2024 年通过股票押注赢得了大笔奖金。比尔·盖茨的前助手身家达 1540 亿美元，很快就会比微软联合创始人霍华德·舒尔茨更富有。沃伦·巴菲特刚刚罕见地去了一趟东京。

sentences.zh: 这是一个灾难性的寿司晚餐的故事，这使他发誓永远不再吃日本菜。21个州在失业率飙升后敲响了经济衰退的钟声沃伦·巴菲特正在建造应急基金的诺亚方舟。

sentences.en: Here's why he's stacked up more than $300 billion.The 'Shark Tank' star Kevin O'Leary warns couples not to combine finances: 'I don't care how in love you are'The Waltons are once again the world's wealthiest family, beating out Gulf royalty and fashion dynasties
sentences.zh: 这就是他财富超过 3000 亿美元的原因。《创智赢家》明星凯文·奥利里警告夫妇不要合并财务：“我不在乎你们有多相爱”沃尔顿家族再次成为世界上最富有的家族，击败了海湾皇室和时尚王朝

content_html_zh:
马克·库班预测人工智能将让工人使用“像飞行员和赛车手一样”的模拟器进行工作培训

作者：Theron Mohamed 您目前正在关注该作者！ 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。

几十年来，努力获取工作经验和向同事学习一直是建立成功职业生涯的关键。 马克·库班表示，这种情况肯定会改变。

这位科技亿万富翁和前“创智赢家”投资者周四在 X 帖子中表示，他预计“下一个伟大的人工智能应用程序”将是“工作模拟器”。

“员工在未来人工智能世界中获得经验的方式将与今天大不相同，”他写道。

“员工在公司不会有那么多接触点来获取知识和经验。 这就是历史上判断的来源。”

库班预测，“就像赛车手和飞行员一样”，未来几代工人将接受模拟培训，旨在教他们如何应对工作场所的现实场景。

商业飞行员在训练期间会在模拟器上花费很长时间，而近年来，赛车手无需在赛道上进行训练的模拟器也变得越来越普遍。

库班表示，“聪明的公司会让拥有最多领域知识的员工和利益相关者创建模拟器，引导他们应对可能面临的各种情况，并帮助他们做好准备。”

“入职将具有完全不同的含义，”他补充道。

由开源或开放权重驱动的下一个伟大的人工智能应用程序将是工作模拟器。 员工在未来人工智能世界中获得经验的方式将与今天大不相同。 员工在公司不会有那么多的接触点来获取知识……——马克·库班 (@mcuban) 2026 年 7 月 24 日

很容易想象一位新晋合格的律师坐在模拟证词、法庭听证会和和解谈判中，以便他们知道会发生什么。

或者实习医生通过与人工智能患者交谈、协助虚拟手术以及参与模拟紧急情况来为现实生活中的情况做好准备。

如果像库班建议的那样，这些场景是通过利用这些律师事务所和医院现实生活中同事的知识和经验来创建的，那么工作模拟器就可以提供实用、现实的培训。

库班本周多次表示，人工智能将改变世界，但在“阅读房间”和情商等领域存在不足。 他说它与人类合作效果最好。

“将人工智能生产力与人类的实时能力和判断力相结合，你将从这两项投资中获得最大的回报，”他在周四发帖称。

阅读下一篇

Theron Mohamed 您目前正在关注该作者！ 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。

塞隆·穆罕默德 (Theron Mohamed) 是《商业内幕》趋势团队驻伦敦记者。 他的报道领域涵盖金融、投资、财富、市场和经济。Theron 于 2019 年加入 BI，担任 Markets Insider 记者，并晋升为通讯员，并于 2024 年加入趋势团队。 他此前曾为《投资者纪事》杂志报道科技、媒体和电信股票，并曾在《金融时报》数据团队短暂任职。 他在纽约《华尔街日报》实习，主要为《Heard on the Street》撰稿。塞隆曾为《独立报》、《电讯报》、《连线》和几家小型出版物担任自由撰稿人。 他拥有伦敦经济学院地理学学士学位和哥伦比亚大学新闻学硕士学位。塞隆经常报道沃伦·巴菲特、迈克尔·伯里、杰里米·格兰瑟姆和其他顶级投资者。 他还撰写有关世界上最富有的人的文章，并分享来自各种富有和成功人士的财务建议。向塞隆发送电子邮件至 tmohamed@businessinsider.com，并在 X @theron_mohamed.Expertise 上关注他
